PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE PURSUANT TO CCP 170.6(A)(2)TO JUDGE PETER BUSCH FILED BY PLAINTIFF SAVEMUNI.COM, AN UNINCORPORATED ASSOCIATION OF INDIVIDUALS WONG, HOWARD A SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA RESIDENT AND TAXPAYER

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS HAVE TIMELY FILED A CHALLENGE PURSUANT TO CCP 170.6 AGAINST JUDGE PETER J. BUSCH. THEREFORE, THE HEARING ON DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO RECREATION AND PARK COMMISSION, MAYOR EDWIN M. LEE IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS MAYOR OF SAN FRANCISCO, BOARD OF SUPERVISORS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND RPD DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS DENNIS KERN IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 26, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302 (TO BE HEARD BY JUDGE MILLER). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO RECREATION AND PARK COMMISSION, MAYOR EDWIN M. LEE IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS, BOARD OF SUPERVISORS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, RPD DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS DENNIS KERN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - PLAINTIFFS' UNOPPOSED REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED EXCEPT AS TO EXHIBIT L AS TO WHICH THERE IS A DISPUTE ABOUT ITS AUTHENTICITY. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND PURSUANT CIVIL PROCEDURE CODE SECTION 430.10(E) FOR FAILURE TO STATE FACTS NECESSARY TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER APPLICABLE LAW. THE PROPOSED SUBWAY STATION IS NOT A STRUCTURE FOR "NON-RECREATIONAL PURPOSES" WITHIN THE MEANING OF SECTION 4.113(2) OF THE SAN FRANCISCO CITY CHARTER. THE PROPOSED SUBWAY STATION AT UNION SQUARE PARK INCIDENTALLY PROMOTES PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE PARK, AND IS A RECREATIONAL PURPOSE CONSISTENT WITH PARK PURPOSES AND PUBLIC ENJOYMENT OF THE PARK. HUMPHREYS V. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (1928) 92 CAL. APP. 2D 69. CITIZENS PLANNING ASSN. V. CITY OF SANTA BARBARA (2011) 191 CAL. APP. 4TH 1541, UPON WHICH PLAINTIFFS RELY, IS DISTINGUISHABLE. THAT CASE TURNED ON THE FACT THAT THE PROPOSED USE OF PARK LAND WAS TO PROVIDE ACCESS TO A PRIVATE RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT, WHICH IS NOT THE CASE HERE. THE MARCH 12 HEARING ON DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR BY THE MOVING PARTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-27-2013 Continued To Apr-24-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2013 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS N. LIPPE OF LIPPE GAFFNEY WAGNER LLP, TEL. NO. 777-5600 X 202 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. WAYNE K. SNODGRASS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4675 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
ATTY. STEPHEN N. ROBERTS OF NOSSAMAN LLP, TEL. NO. 438-7213 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO RECREATION AND PARK COMMISSION, MAYOR EDWIN M. LEE IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS, BOARD OF SUPERVISORS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, RPD DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS DENNIS KERN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Plaintiffs' unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted except as to Exhibit L as to which there is a dispute about its authenticity. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend pursuant Civil Procedure Code section 430.10(e) for failure to state facts necessary to constitute a cause of action under applicable law. The proposed subway station is not a structure for "non-recreational purposes" within the meaning of Section 4.113(2) of the San Francisco City Charter. The proposed subway station at Union Square Park incidentally promotes public access to the park, and is a recreational purpose consistent with park purposes and public enjoyment of the park. Humphreys v. City and County of San Francisco (1928) 92 Cal. App. 2d 69. Citizens Planning Assn. v. City of Santa Barbara (2011) 191 Cal. App. 4th 1541, upon which Plaintiffs rely, is distinguishable. That case turned on the fact that the proposed use of park land was to provide access to a private residential development, which is not the case here. The March 12 hearing on defendant's demurrer is taken off calendar by the moving party. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
